summary: Recent changes to the WebRTC web API enhance its stability and efficiency, particularly in handling connections. The clearing of the cache side effect of TS_NONE improves how the system manages inactive transport connections, ensuring that closed updates are appropriately communicated, even if those transports were never active. Additionally, enabling transport-cc for audio codecs optimizes how audio quality is monitored and adapted during calls. These updates lead to a more reliable and streamlined experience for users engaging in activities like video conferencing or online gaming, where real-time performance is crucial. **Overall, these improvements contribute to a smoother and more efficient WebRTC experience, ultimately benefiting users relying on this technology for communication.**
